title: Better Music for Menu&Discs
description: Upgraded version of the nostalgic Minecraft music for in the menu screen and in the game!
body:
![logo](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1d2c2e41a8d3bcdb658cfc1fe1a77579e5e8f289.png)

In this pack, you'll experience upgraded versions of many of the game's music tracks. This pack includes the music discs, almost all of which already have a different version—the EPIC version! The three most dreadful discs (3, 5, and 11) have also been replaced with different music (see the table). Several background songs have also been replaced with the epic version.

But the reason I originally started this resource pack was for the "menu" songs (the menu screen). I wanted to create a resource pack with customized songs for starting Minecraft Java Edition. After a few tries, I succeeded! However, because above 1.20.1, a song doesn't immediately or automatically play on the menu screen when you start the game, I was often confused about whether it was working or not. So don't panic if you don't hear a song on the menu screen, it will appear in 3 minutes or go into the game again.

**_Credits:_**
Most of the music for this resource pack comes from the YouTube channel @Kalamity_Music. There are two reasons I chose her music: first, because it's very adventurous and I also find it easy to blend with your own thoughts and memories. The second reason is very simple... almost no other people could make this music as well as she could, so that's why. Thank you so much for letting me use the music of these people: @Kalamity_Music, @J0sh2k, @andreaszoeller, @SynomaticCanvas, @luffimusic, @I_am_Angerer.

And especially @Kalamity_Music (also Kalamity), because thanks to her, it was very easy to find the right music for this resource pack.

![Discs and Menu](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/db618b18439abf9b27478111e1402cecfec13dcb.png)

![While playing](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b723c09b4fe835e6802e82bc8e47c27f9c4ecc3f.png)


Disclaimer
This Minecraft resource pack replaces various in-game music tracks with alternative or “epic” versions.

The original Minecraft soundtrack was composed by multiple artists (including C418 and other official Minecraft composers). All original compositions and recordings remain the property of their respective copyright holders.

The audio used in this pack consists of alternative versions of these tracks that were found on YouTube and are used only to change the in-game listening experience. I do not claim ownership of any of the original music, melodies, or recordings.

This is a fan-made project and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or approved by Mojang Studios or Microsoft.

If you are a copyright holder and want your content credited, changed, or removed, please contact me and I will update or remove it as soon as possible.
